qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Published query-embed API leaks non-public note content to read-only users
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets POST /api/search/searchEmbedBlock and an attacker-controlled SQL-to-IAL metadata leak in publish filtering, while the advisory is a path traversal in kernel/server/serve.go serveExport() caused by a second url.PathUnescape() on /export/ paths.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The only qualifying finding is an open-world disclosure in the published searchEmbedBlock flow rather than the advisory's double-decoded /export/ path traversal in serveExport().
evidence: The advisory describes /export/ path traversal in kernel/server/serve.go serveExport() via a redundant url.PathUnescape() call on already-decoded request paths.
The only qualifying finding report describes POST /api/search/searchEmbedBlock, attacker-controlled SQL, and leakage through blocks[].block.ial after publish filtering.
The finding's cited code locations are router.go, search.go, block.go, publish_access.go, and filetree.go, with no overlap with the advisory's serveExport() path handling.
